first_paragraph: The [open water swimming](/wiki/Open_water_swimming_at_the_2011_World_Aquatics_Championships "Open water swimming at the 2011 World Aquatics Championships") **team** event at the [2011 World Aquatics Championships](/wiki/2011_World_Aquatics_Championships "2011 World Aquatics Championships") was held on 21 July 2011 off the coast of Jinshan City Beach in [Jinshan, Shanghai](/wiki/Jinshan,_Shanghai "Jinshan, Shanghai"), China.

The [open water swimming](/wiki/Open_water_swimming_at_the_2011_World_Aquatics_Championships "Open water swimming at the 2011 World Aquatics Championships") **team** event at the [2011 World Aquatics Championships](/wiki/2011_World_Aquatics_Championships "2011 World Aquatics Championships") was held on 21 July 2011 off the coast of Jinshan City Beach in [Jinshan, Shanghai](/wiki/Jinshan,_Shanghai "Jinshan, Shanghai"), China.

The [United States](/wiki/United_States_at_the_2011_World_Aquatics_Championships "United States at the 2011 World Aquatics Championships") won gold with a time of 57:00.6, while [Australia](/wiki/Australia_at_the_2011_World_Aquatics_Championships "Australia at the 2011 World Aquatics Championships") won silver with 57:01.8, and [Germany](/wiki/Germany_at_the_2011_World_Aquatics_Championships "Germany at the 2011 World Aquatics Championships") won bronze with 57:44.2.

## Event description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Open_water_swimming_at_the_2011_World_Aquatics_Championships_%E2%80%93_Team&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Event description")]

It was the first time this event took place at a world championship.[[1]](#cite_note-:1-1)[[2]](#cite_note-:2-2)

Each country was represented by three swimmers who swam as a team. Each team swam the same 5-kilometre double-loop, with each member of the team starting at the same time. The time it took the slowest member of the team to complete the course determined the team's time, which determined the finishing positions as in a [time trial](/wiki/Time_trial "Time trial"). A staggered start was implemented, meaning each team was set off individually one minute after the previous team.[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3)[[1]](#cite_note-:1-1)

Teams were required to include at least one man and one woman.[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3)

## Race

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Open_water_swimming_at_the_2011_World_Aquatics_Championships_%E2%80%93_Team&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Race")]

The race started at 09:00 [CST](/wiki/Time_in_China "Time in China") on 21 July.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-:3-5) It took place off the coast of Jinshan City Beach in [Jinshan, Shanghai](/wiki/Jinshan,_Shanghai "Jinshan, Shanghai"), China.[[5]](#cite_note-:3-5)

The [United States](/wiki/United_States_at_the_2011_World_Aquatics_Championships "United States at the 2011 World Aquatics Championships") swam in a single file formation, led by [Andrew Gemmell](/wiki/Andrew_Gemmell "Andrew Gemmell") and tailed by [Ashley Twichell](/wiki/Ashley_Twichell "Ashley Twichell"), for the entire race to make use of [drafting](/wiki/Drafting_(aerodynamics) "Drafting (aerodynamics)").[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3) The [Australians](/wiki/Australia_at_the_2011_World_Aquatics_Championships "Australia at the 2011 World Aquatics Championships") spread out a bit more, swimming nearer each other's hips.[[2]](#cite_note-:2-2)

At the end of the first lap, the United States had the fastest split, followed by [Germany](/wiki/Germany_at_the_2011_World_Aquatics_Championships "Germany at the 2011 World Aquatics Championships") and then Australia.[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3) The US won gold with a time of 57:00.6.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3) Australia won silver with 57:01.8, and Germany won bronze with 57:44.2.[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3)[[1]](#cite_note-:1-1)

After the race, Gemmell said "It was pretty incredible for a 5km race to come down to a single second for two teams that started 10 minutes apart. It just shows you how tight the race was. We did a great job working together as a team."[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3)
